name: Somers General
signature: A tiny, perfectly curated general store and café in sleepy Somers — sourdough, pastries, cheese, pantry goods, Peninsula wines, and a weekend brunch menu
editorNote:
Somers General is the Peninsula's best kept secret in retail form. The room is small, the shelves are tightly curated — Peninsula pantry goods, a cheese counter, a wine shelf that leans local, pastries and sourdough that would hold their own on Mornington's Main Street — and the weekend brunch menu is one of the quiet highlights of the Balnarring side of the Peninsula.

What makes the place special is that it feels like it should not exist. Somers is small, sleepy, and almost never on the day-trip list. Finding a genuine general store here that is punching well above its postcode is one of those small Peninsula discoveries people return to every trip.

Drive out on a Sunday morning. Stay for the brunch. Leave with a basket.
